% ===== Geometric backbone of Sub-brick A: decomposition of lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma =====
% The identification of the degree-p {\v C}ech-nerve object with the coproduct of intersection
% opens is the single hardest leaf of the augmented-{\v C}ech-resolution lane. It is split here
% into a \uses-linked chain: (1) unfold the nerve object to a wide pullback [mechanical];
% (2) coproduct distributes over the wide fibre power [the genuine gap, multi-cycle, abstract for
% FinitaryPreExtensive then instantiated at Scheme]; (3) the wide fibre power of open immersions
% is the intersection open [mechanical, iterating isPullback_opens_inf]; (4) assemble. The Mathlib
% facts each step rests on are recorded as \mathlibok anchors in the dependency-anchor group below.

\begin{lemma}
[The degree-\(p\) {\v C}ech-nerve object is a wide pullback]
  \label{lem:cechBackbone_obj_widePullback}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechBackbone_obj_widePullback}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet; the name is the prover's target.
  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}
  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an open cover with cover arrow
  \(q = \operatorname{Sigma.desc}\mathcal{U}.f : \coprod_{i \in I} U_i \to X\). For every
  \(p\), the underlying scheme of the degree-\(p\) object
  \((\operatorname{coverCechNerveOver}\mathcal{U}).\mathrm{obj}\,[p]\) of the {\v C}ech nerve of
  Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} is the wide pullback
  \[
    \operatorname{widePullback} X \;\bigl(\,\lambda\,k : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1).\ \textstyle\coprod_{i} U_i\,\bigr)
      \;\bigl(\,\lambda\,k.\ q\,\bigr),
  \]
  the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power of \(q\) over \(X\), with structure map to \(X\) the canonical
  \(\operatorname{WidePullback.base}\). Equivalently, in \(\operatorname{Over} X\) the object is
  \(\operatorname{Over.mk}(\operatorname{WidePullback.base})\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}
  Pure unfolding of the definitions. By Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve},
  \(\operatorname{coverCechNerveOver}\mathcal{U}\) is the lift into \(\operatorname{Over} X\) of
  \(\operatorname{coverCechNerve}\mathcal{U} = (\operatorname{coverArrow}\mathcal{U}).
  \operatorname{augmentedCechNerve}\) along its augmentation. The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an
  arrow \(f\) has, in degree \(n\), the object \(\operatorname{Arrow.cechNerve} f\,.\mathrm{obj}\,
  n = \operatorname{widePullback} f.\mathrm{right}\,(\lambda\,k : \mathrm{Fin}(n+1).\ f.\mathrm{left})
  \,(\lambda\,k.\ f.\mathrm{hom})\). Here \(\operatorname{coverArrow}\mathcal{U} =
  \operatorname{Arrow.mk}(q)\) with \(\mathrm{right} = X\), \(\mathrm{left} = \coprod_i U_i\) and
  \(\mathrm{hom} = q\), so the degree-\(p\) object is exactly the displayed wide pullback, and the
  \(\operatorname{Over.lift}\) records its structure map to \(X\) as
  \(\operatorname{WidePullback.base}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Coproduct distributes over the wide fibre power]
  \label{lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.FinitaryPreExtensive.widePullback_coproduct_iso}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet; the name is the prover's target.
  % This is the genuine Mathlib gap of the chain (gap C of analogies/stub1-scheme-coproduct.md):
  % the WIDE/iterated distributivity is absent from Mathlib and is a multi-cycle build on its own.
  \uses{lem:isIso_sigmaDesc_map_mathlib, lem:sigmaSigmaIso_mathlib,
    lem:widePullbackCone_isLimitOfFan_mathlib, lem:finitaryExtensive_scheme_mathlib}
  Let \(\mathcal{C}\) be a category with pullbacks that is finitary pre-extensive
  (\(\operatorname{FinitaryPreExtensive}\mathcal{C}\)), let \(\iota\) be a finite index type,
  \(S \in \mathcal{C}\), and \((X_i \to S)_{i \in \iota}\) a finite family over \(S\). Then for every
  \(p\) the \((p+1)\)-fold wide fibre power of the coproduct map \(\coprod_i X_i \to S\) over \(S\)
  decomposes as a coproduct over multi-indices:
  \[
    \bigl(\textstyle\coprod_{i} X_i\bigr)^{\times_S (p+1)}
      \;\cong\;
    \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}
      \bigl(X_{\sigma(0)} \times_S \cdots \times_S X_{\sigma(p)}\bigr),
  \]
  the \(\sigma\)-component being the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power of the legs \(X_{\sigma(k)} \to S\)
  over \(S\), and the structure map of the coproduct being
  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\) of the component structure maps. The result is to be stated
  abstractly for \(\operatorname{FinitaryPreExtensive}\mathcal{C}\) (reusable and Mathlib-aligned)
  and then instantiated at \(\mathcal{C} = \operatorname{Scheme}\), which is finitary extensive by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:finitaryExtensive_scheme_mathlib}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:isIso_sigmaDesc_map_mathlib, lem:sigmaSigmaIso_mathlib,
    lem:widePullbackCone_isLimitOfFan_mathlib}
  Induction on \(p\). Reformulate the wide pullback over \(S\) as a product in \(\operatorname{Over}
  S\): the wide pullback over a base is, by Lemma~\ref{lem:widePullbackCone_isLimitOfFan_mathlib},
  the product of its legs in the slice (the base \(\operatorname{Over.mk}(\mathrm{id}_S)\) being
  terminal), so the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power over \(S\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold product in
  \(\operatorname{Over} S\) and the induction has the clean recursion
  \(P^{\times (p+2)} = P \times_S P^{\times (p+1)}\) along \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+2) \cong \mathrm{Fin}(1)
  \oplus \mathrm{Fin}(p+1)\).

  \emph{Base step} \(p = 0\): the \(1\)-fold fibre power is \(\coprod_i X_i\) itself, indexed by
  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(1) \to \iota\), i.e.\ by \(\iota\) --- the trivial reindexing.

  \emph{Inductive step}: assume \(\bigl(\coprod_i X_i\bigr)^{\times_S (p+1)} \cong \coprod_{\tau :
  \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota} \bigl(X_{\tau(0)} \times_S \cdots \times_S X_{\tau(p)}\bigr)\). Then
  \[
    \bigl(\textstyle\coprod_i X_i\bigr)^{\times_S (p+2)}
      \;\cong\;
    \bigl(\textstyle\coprod_i X_i\bigr) \times_S \bigl(\textstyle\coprod_i X_i\bigr)^{\times_S (p+1)}
      \;\cong\;
    \bigl(\textstyle\coprod_i X_i\bigr) \times_S
      \bigl(\textstyle\coprod_\tau Y_\tau\bigr),
  \]
  where \(Y_\tau = X_{\tau(0)} \times_S \cdots \times_S X_{\tau(p)}\). The one-sided
  distributivity of the coproduct over the fibre product --- the instance
  Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_sigmaDesc_map_mathlib} applied with the second family the singleton
  \(\{\coprod_\tau Y_\tau\}\), then with the first family the singleton --- turns the right-hand
  side into \(\coprod_{i}\coprod_{\tau} \bigl(X_i \times_S Y_\tau\bigr)\). Flattening the nested
  coproduct with \(\operatorname{sigmaSigmaIso}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:sigmaSigmaIso_mathlib} re-indexes \(\coprod_i \coprod_\tau\) as a single coproduct
  over pairs \((i, \tau)\), which is precisely \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+2) \to \iota\) via
  \(\sigma = \operatorname{Fin.cons} i\,\tau\); under this bijection \(X_i \times_S Y_\tau =
  X_{\sigma(0)} \times_S \cdots \times_S X_{\sigma(p+1)}\). The structure maps agree because each
  distributivity isomorphism commutes with the projection to \(S\) by construction. This is a
  multi-cycle build: the bookkeeping of the slice-product reformulation and the
  \(\operatorname{Fin.cons}\) reindexing across the induction is the bulk of the work.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[The wide fibre power of open immersions is the intersection open]
  \label{lem:widePullback_openImm_inter}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.widePullback_openImm_inter}
  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet; the name is the prover's target.
  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, lem:isPullback_opens_inf_mathlib}
  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an open cover and
  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I\) a multi-index. The \((p+1)\)-fold wide fibre power over
  \(X\) of the open immersions \((U_{\sigma(k)} \hookrightarrow X)_{k}\) is the intersection open:
  \[
    \operatorname{widePullback} X \;(\lambda\,k.\ U_{\sigma(k)})\;(\lambda\,k.\ (U_{\sigma(k)}).\iota)
      \;\cong\;
    (\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma).\mathrm{toScheme}
      \;=\; \bigl(\textstyle\bigcap_k U_{\sigma(k)}\bigr).\mathrm{toScheme},
  \]
  over \(X\), and the structure map is the open immersion
  \(j_\sigma : \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma \hookrightarrow X\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:isPullback_opens_inf_mathlib, def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
  Induction on the family, iterating the binary case. The pullback over \(X\) of two open
  immersions \(U.\iota, V.\iota\) is the intersection open \((U \sqcap V).\mathrm{toScheme}\) with
  the two \(\operatorname{homOfLE}\) inclusions --- this is
  \(\operatorname{isPullback\_opens\_inf}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:isPullback_opens_inf_mathlib}. The
  wide pullback of the family \((U_{\sigma(k)})_{k}\) is the iterated binary pullback over \(X\)
  along \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+1)\), so iterating the binary intersection gives
  \(\bigsqcap_k U_{\sigma(k)}\), which is the indexed infimum
  \(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma = \bigsqcap_k \operatorname{coverOpen}
  \mathcal{U}\,(\sigma(k))\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} (recall
  \(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i = (\mathcal{U}.f\,i).\operatorname{opensRange}\) is the
  image open \(U_i\)). The structure map to \(X\) is the inclusion of the intersection, namely
  \(j_\sigma\).
\end{proof}
